body:
Title: CRA 2025 T1 federal tax on taxable income.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package/5000-r.html
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5000-r/5000-r-25e.txt

CRA Form 5000-R Income Tax and Benefit Return for 2025 Step 5 Part A calculates federal tax on taxable income. The form uses the amount from taxable income line 26000 to select one of five columns.

Column 1 applies if line 26000 is $57,375 or less. Line 71 is $0.00, line 73 is 14.5%, line 75 is $0.00, and line 76 is line 74 plus line 75.

Column 2 applies if line 26000 is more than $57,375 but not more than $114,750. Line 71 is $57,375.00, line 73 is 20.5%, line 75 is $8,319.38, and line 76 is line 74 plus line 75.

Column 3 applies if line 26000 is more than $114,750 but not more than $177,882. Line 71 is $114,750.00, line 73 is 26%, line 75 is $20,081.25, and line 76 is line 74 plus line 75.

Column 4 applies if line 26000 is more than $177,882 but not more than $253,414. Line 71 is $177,882.00, line 73 is 29%, line 75 is $36,495.57, and line 76 is line 74 plus line 75.

Column 5 applies if line 26000 is more than $253,414. Line 71 is $253,414.00, line 73 is 33%, line 75 is $58,399.85, and line 76 is line 74 plus line 75.

For each column, line 70 is the amount from line 26000, line 72 is line 70 minus line 71 and cannot be negative, line 74 is line 72 multiplied by the percentage from line 73, and line 76 is federal tax on taxable income. The form directs the taxpayer to enter the amount from line 76 on line 119.
